reasoning: The base rate for a stock price to increase over a typical 1-week period is approximately 52%-54%. Lennar (LEN) just released its Q1 2026 earnings on March 12, 2026, after the market closed. The stock had experienced a heavy selloff leading up to the report (falling from ~$116 in late February to $92.54 on March 12). However, upon the post-earnings market open and earnings call on March 13, the stock staged a rally, closing at $94.96. With the major risk event (earnings report) now in the rearview mirror, and a positive initial reaction on the first trading day post-earnings, the stock's performance between March 13 and March 20 (the effective trading days for the March 15 and March 22 weekend resolution dates) is expected to resume standard market drift behavior, perhaps with a slight upward bias due to standard weekly drift and potential mean reversion after a steep prior selloff. The time-series statistical tool gives a 61% estimate for exceeding the $94.96 threshold, but we lean closer to the natural market base rate, estimating a 54% chance the price closes higher on March 20 than it did on March 13.
